but because it happened the way it happened, unfortunately for me it truly ruined will smith ever being in anything, cause all i can picture is him sim*ing c*cking for his cheating narcissistic man acting wife, 
all i can think of is them talking about entanglements, how will smith is creepaly similar to adam 22 from no jumper, how everytime he speaks in a movie, especially when he is talking s**t to say a criminal, i just imagine he is gonna start yelling about keeping his f**king wife's name, out thier f**king month's!!!! 

An that if very unfortunate for me, because as awesome as Martin Laurence is, as much as i liked the older bad boy films, as much as i liked a lot of other movie's will himself has been in, like- independence day & i am legend, (witch i wish we had got a sequel for a long time ago), 
I just can't watch anymore film's he is in, i can't support it.
textOriginal:
Man, i use to be a big fan of wills, but now i just really hate the fact he did what he did when & where he did it, because if it wasn't during such a big event, it probably wouldn't have been recorded, or if it was, it probably wouldn't have been picked up by the news like it was, an we could have just pretended it never happened, or just legit forgot all about it,
but because it happened the way it happened, unfortunately for me it truly ruined will smith ever being in anything, cause all i can picture is him sim*ing c*cking for his cheating narcissistic man acting wife, 
all i can think of is them talking about entanglements, how will smith is creepaly similar to adam 22 from no jumper, how everytime he speaks in a movie, especially when he is talking s**t to say a criminal, i just imagine he is gonna start yelling about keeping his f**king wife's name, out thier f**king month's!!!! 

An that if very unfortunate for me, because as awesome as Martin Laurence is, as much as i liked the older bad boy films, as much as i liked a lot of other movie's will himself has been in, like- independence day & i am legend, (witch i wish we had got a sequel for a long time ago), 
I just can't watch anymore film's he is in, i can't support it.

textDisplay:
Killing off Captain Howard was a severe miscalculation. He was the funniest and most likable character in the franchise and killing him off wasn't raising the stakes,you kill him... THERE ARE LITERALLY NO STAKES LEFT! 


Plus Joey Pants deserved a second wind, his career died after Sopranos and the second Bad boys. Still making him integral and what drives the plot plus bringing him back a'la Randy in Scream is a nice touch. 

And it seems time+context is important in accepting actors and their major flaws. I could not watch Emancipation. This however, I'm on board with,it looks entertaining...oh,and Mikes son who blew the captains face off is out apparently and he's cracking jokes. 

Mmm, that's actually the only problematic aspect in this.
textOriginal:
Killing off Captain Howard was a severe miscalculation. He was the funniest and most likable character in the franchise and killing him off wasn't raising the stakes,you kill him... THERE ARE LITERALLY NO STAKES LEFT! 


Plus Joey Pants deserved a second wind, his career died after Sopranos and the second Bad boys. Still making him integral and what drives the plot plus bringing him back a'la Randy in Scream is a nice touch. 

And it seems time+context is important in accepting actors and their major flaws. I could not watch Emancipation. This however, I'm on board with,it looks entertaining...oh,and Mikes son who blew the captains face off is out apparently and he's cracking jokes. 

Mmm, that's actually the only problematic aspect in this.

textDisplay: 48 Hours. It basically set the blueprint for what we identify as the modern buddy cop movies. The Nice Guys, Zootopia, The Other Guys, Men in Black, Die Hard with a Vengeance, Freebie and the Bean, and The French Connection are really great takes on the genre too. If you're considering buddy cop movies that aren't also comedies, then End of Watch, Miami Vice, Training Day, Cop Land, Se7en, Point Break, Alien Nation, Colors, In the Heat of the Night, and Stray Dog are must watch.
textOriginal: 48 Hours. It basically set the blueprint for what we identify as the modern buddy cop movies. The Nice Guys, Zootopia, The Other Guys, Men in Black, Die Hard with a Vengeance, Freebie and the Bean, and The French Connection are really great takes on the genre too. If you're considering buddy cop movies that aren't also comedies, then End of Watch, Miami Vice, Training Day, Cop Land, Se7en, Point Break, Alien Nation, Colors, In the Heat of the Night, and Stray Dog are must watch.

textDisplay: This movie looks BAD. The first time I saw the beginning of this trailer (the convenience store bit) it was very close to the Super Bowl so by the looks of it I just assumed it was some funny Super Bowl commercial, because it definitely doesn't look like an actual movie. The directors of this film seem to try their hardest to come up with "innovative" shots, but the whole thing looks messy as hell from a visual standpoint. As much as I'm not a fan of Michael Bay, at theast that man knows what he does and it works.
textOriginal: This movie looks BAD. The first time I saw the beginning of this trailer (the convenience store bit) it was very close to the Super Bowl so by the looks of it I just assumed it was some funny Super Bowl commercial, because it definitely doesn't look like an actual movie. The directors of this film seem to try their hardest to come up with "innovative" shots, but the whole thing looks messy as hell from a visual standpoint. As much as I'm not a fan of Michael Bay, at theast that man knows what he does and it works.

textDisplay: @shreyvr6631 The actor who played Aladdin is Mena Massoud. So Aladdin made a billion worldwide, and the actor Mena who plays the main character complained about not getting any roles and believes it's based on race. The actor Will Smith went out of his way to come out and say it has nothing to do with race that he is exagerrating and tried to use himself as an example. The reason why what Will did shouldn't count because he already has a fanbase before he started doing movies of course he is going to be getting movie roles easily. The actor who plays Mena didn't get a movie role until 3 years after Aladdin. I find it wrong if you are part of a minority group and you're going to try to downplay prejudice against another minority group or aka being racist then that makes you much worst then a white racist. White racist people have an excuse they were raised that way, but when a black person does it it's like they didn't learned anything from their history or thought that behavior is somehow okay. What Will did was a form of racism against a struggling actor and is way WAY WORSE then the slapping incident.

textDisplay:
*WILL SMITH SLAPPED CHRIS ROCK at the 2022 OSCARS ceremony*
*The reason: a joke from the movie G.I JANE*

Jada Pinkett Smith has been separated from Will Smith for 7 years; her separation from the actor occurred in 2016. “I don't call what I do with Will a marriage, we are a partnership.”
“Jada and I have decided to never say that we are a married couple again,”
Source: Jada Pinkett Smith: Worthy book

textDisplay: The last edition to the franchise was great, so I’m excited for this one! Sony or the production companies, if you’re reading this, and you want to make an universe, we need a spin-off with Mike’s son, Armando. The actor who portrayed him was phenomenal! If you like my idea, make is a back door spin-off, don’t market is as one. Let the audience figure it out when we buy the ticket and we’re either at the end or halfway through the movie! Given you can’t market it as one, we need big names to join the film, but not the usual suspects. If you’re thinking “the Rock,” let’s make him the bad guy since he’s always good. You’re welcome!
textOriginal: The last edition to the franchise was great, so I’m excited for this one! Sony or the production companies, if you’re reading this, and you want to make an universe, we need a spin-off with Mike’s son, Armando. The actor who portrayed him was phenomenal! If you like my idea, make is a back door spin-off, don’t market is as one. Let the audience figure it out when we buy the ticket and we’re either at the end or halfway through the movie! Given you can’t market it as one, we need big names to join the film, but not the usual suspects. If you’re thinking “the Rock,” let’s make him the bad guy since he’s always good. You’re welcome!
